mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.65% signals accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 145,554 thousand suggests near-threshold employment level
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive payroll signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 472,142M and HOUST at 1,226K require baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite dual demand contraction signal, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) crossing above 105.0 signals accelerating supply-side capacity utilization that supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 104.5548 is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 105.0 crossings are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.2% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.8% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.4%) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.65% is well below trigger, current CPIAUCSL at 244.158 requires momentum analysis
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite tightening signal, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it is strongest in early-to-mid cycle expansions and weakens in late-cycle periods.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16804.8B requires quarterly comparison for growth rate
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accelerating growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 > 2.5% growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in early-cycle regimes, or mechanism reverses to negative direction in late-cycle periods without explicit regime switch.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,100 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness that presages earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,226K is above trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or housing market contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when HOUST < 1,100K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
